Source: Harbor Freight Warrior heat gun SKU 56434
Notes: Tear down heat gun and measure element resistance and dims; design heater can around it (DR-002). A1 rejected — element will be extracted and mounted in custom heater can. Local pickup at Harbor Freight

Spec: Universal AC ~1-4A draw at 120V; bypass / two-stage cooling (separate cooling impeller from working impeller)
Source: Salvage — junk shop vac / Habitat ReStore / curb
Notes: DR-011 supersedes DR-003. CRITICAL: must be bypass-cooled — flow-through motors shed brush carbon into bean airstream. Inspect dual-airpath separation before committing. Speed control via BLW-CTRL-001 TRIAC dimmer; airflow interlock via CT-001. Frame must be earth-bonded (BOND-001). Static-pressure headroom is huge (20-80in WC) — characterize at TP-001 to find operating duty-cycle range, not whether it meets spec

Spec: AC light dimmer module 8A with on-board zero-cross detector; logic-level PWM input + ZC output (RobotDyn-style)
Source: Amazon — search 'RobotDyn AC dimmer module 8A zero cross Arduino'
Notes: DR-011: ESP32 GPIO 23 → PWM in (gate trigger); GPIO 4 ← ZC out (interrupt). Verify board has both PWM and ZC pins broken out (some only expose PWM). Validate ASIN at order time

Spec: ZMCT103C 5A split-core CT module with burden + bias network
Notes: DR-011: replaces implicit blower_is_running() check from DR-003. Clamp on one motor lead. Output AC riding mid-rail to ESP32 GPIO 34 ADC; firmware computes RMS for airflow interlock threshold. Many breakouts include the bias network — pick one that does

Spec: X-cap line-to-neutral + Y-caps to earth; 6-10A inline module
Source: Amazon — search 'AC line filter EMI 10A inline' or salvage from PC PSU input stage
Notes: DR-011: contains conducted EMI from TRIAC switching + brushed-motor commutation. Mandatory for SPI signal integrity. Salvage from a dead PC PSU input stage is a fine option

Spec: Mixed-bore clamp-on ferrite cores (4mm-10mm cable diameters); set of 5-10
Source: Amazon — search 'snap on ferrite cores assortment'
Notes: DR-011: install on motor leads (between TRIAC and motor) and on every TC SPI cable run. Brushed motor + TRIAC switching is a significant EMI source — E13 shielded cable is necessary but not sufficient on its own

Spec: M4 or #8 ring terminal + matching screw / star washer; green/yellow stranded pigtail 14 AWG
Notes: DR-011: universal motors have nontrivial leakage current — frame must be bonded to mains earth. Ring terminal under a frame screw with a star washer for positive metal-to-metal contact
Drive via NPN buffer from 5V rail (E7); add RC snubber across output (E10); add thermal fuse as independent backup. 3-32V DC input / 24-380V AC out. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07FVR37QN
Hardware WDT enabled in firmware (E1/F2). Single-board listing; 2-pack alt B09KLQF4RR. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0718T232Z
Total ~$12-18; use shielded cable to reduce SPI noise near SSR switching (E13). Order 3 individual units (cheaper than 3-packs). Alt: HUABAN B083R5F3FF. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07K5MJ43M

Post-heater pre-plenum placement; compression fitting mount (M9). 6in long 0.118in (3mm) 316SS sheath quick-disconnect K-type up to 500C. Order qty 3 of this listing for TC-001/002/003. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B078QS3NWP
Side-entry lower chamber; compression fitting mount (M9). Same listing as TC-001. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B078QS3NWP
Exhaust stream above chamber; compression fitting mount (M9). Same listing as TC-001. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B078QS3NWP
Positive sealing and strain relief for TC probes (M9). Pack of 2 — order 2 packs for 4 fittings (3 needed + 1 spare). 304 SS double-ferrule. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B09FF7LRL6
Old phone charger; must be isolated (all modern ones are)
Do not cheap out - must be properly rated. E5: consider 20A circuit + 12 AWG cord to meet NEC 80% continuous rule
E8: Must be double-pole (switches both L and N) to protect against reversed cord polarity. Must be accessible during operation

Mounts on heater can body; independent last-resort cutoff if SSR fails shorted. NEC SEFUSE 240C 10A axial — 228C unavailable from major sellers in singles. 240C is 12C above target but well below nichrome failure; acceptable per safety-critical substitution review. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B015675DA8

E6: Mounts in heated airstream downstream of element. Lower rating than THFUSE-001 and lower thermal lag — trips faster on no-airflow overheat. AUPO BF192 10A axial. 10-pack — keep spares for replacements after trips. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FJ5WJM9Z
E7: Buffers ESP32 3.3V GPIO to 5V for reliable SSR triggering. Kit covers 2N2222 and many other useful TO-92 BJTs. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B071KK9B3H
E7: Limits base current for Q2 NPN buffer. ELEGOO 17-value 1% kit covers a wide range — keep on hand for incidental needs. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B072BL2VX1
E10: RC snubber across SSR output — suppresses inductive spikes. Metal oxide flame-proof axial 5%. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07V2NV3P9
E10: RC snubber across SSR output — must be X2-rated for mains use. 275VAC X2 = equivalent safety class to 400VDC for mains snubber duty. 103K 10mm pitch. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B09JWZ5MWQ

Spec: 2.5 in OD 304 SS exhaust pipe 12 in length
Notes: Standard exhaust size; ~2.37 in ID. T3: L/D ~1.0 — slugging risk; 3.0in may be preferred. Local auto parts pickup

Spec: 3.0 in OD 304 SS exhaust pipe 12 in length
Notes: Standard exhaust size; ~2.87 in ID. T3: L/D ~0.57 — better fluidization geometry. Local auto parts pickup

Spec: 3.0 in OD borosilicate glass cylinder ~8 in (down from 10-12 in target)
Notes: A3: Visual feedback on fluidization and roast development. Borosilicate 3in dia x 8in tall open-end chimney/lampshade. NOTE: 8in is shorter than original 10-12in target — verify expansion-chamber transition still clears bean bed. Handle with care — thermal shock risk. Test alongside SS tubes. 2-pack alt B075ZQFFK4. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B06XJT46QL

Spec: 1/6 size SS steam table pan 4in deep (~6.4x6.3x4in 18/8 SS)
Source: Restaurant supply or thrift store (Amazon backup B083M4N3SJ)
Notes: Right-side-up orientation; drill side-entry hole for heater can (DR-007). M7: Burn off outdoors at full temp for 10 min before first use with beans. Amazon backup is AmazonCommercial 22-gauge 2-pack — restaurant supply or thrift preferred for cost. Link (backup): https://www.amazon.com/dp/B083M4N3SJ

Spec: Flat SS sheet ~1in wide annular ring with 2.37in ID hole
Source: Cut from SS flat plate or sheet
Notes: Bolted to pan rim with 3-4 bolts; seats plate + chamber (DR-008). M3: Use fender washers + backing strips at bolt holes to spread clamping load

Spec: Flat SS sheet ~1in wide annular ring with 2.87in ID hole
Source: Cut from same SS sheet as PLEN-002
Notes: Swap pair with PLATE-001B for 3.0in chamber (DR-008). M3: Use fender washers + backing strips

M8: Changed from ceramic fiber to fiberglass — ceramic fiber sheds respirable particles. High-temp seal between clamping ring and pan rim (DR-008). 7ft is more than needed; 9ft alt B01ETVGA22 / 20ft alt B01ETVSP5W. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B01ETURR0M
M2: Welded or press-fit through pan wall hole. Prevents pan wall deformation under hose clamp load. Use hose clamps inside + outside

Spec: Perforated SS sheet 4in dia disc with 1/16in holes (cut to ~2.37 in)
Notes: Pre-cut 4in 19-gauge 304 SS round disc with 1/16in holes (22% open area). Order qty 2 — one cut to 2.37in for CHAM-001A and one to 2.87in for CHAM-001B. Verify open area gives target dP at 12 CFM during T1 testing. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B09SF15S47

Spec: Perforated SS sheet 4in dia disc with 1/16in holes (cut to ~2.87 in)
Notes: Same listing as PLATE-001A — order 2 of these total. Cut to 2.87in for 3.0in chamber. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B09SF15S47
Bolted/riveted inside plenum opposite side-entry; redirects jet to pan floor (DR-009). T2: If TP-001 shows uneven fluidization add second perforated baffle

Spec: ~4in OD x 5in tall SS cylinder or can
Source: Thrift store / restaurant supply (no good Amazon match)
Notes: Step-up from chamber OD; chaff settles by velocity drop (DR-006). Look for SS flatware caddy / utensil cylinder / coffee tin / SS cup at thrift. No clean Amazon listing matches 4in dia x 5in tall in pure SS at low cost — defer to thrift hunt

Spec: 30 mesh 304 SS woven sheet ~4in dia disc cut
Notes: Cut from sheet; fits inside top of expansion chamber (DR-006). T8: May clog with chaff oils — plan for mid-roast cleaning or test coarser mesh. Sheet is large — plenty for spares. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B09VNSQGVH

Spec: All-stainless worm-drive clamp sized to joints
Notes: M11: Must be all-stainless (not zinc-plated) for temperature tolerance. Covers blower-to-can heater-can-to-plenum and expansion chamber joints

Spec: Sheet metal cone from chamber OD to ~4in expansion chamber OD
Notes: M6: Transition from roast chamber to expansion chamber. Hose clamp at each end
M4: Chamber tube must not be held by gravity alone — vibration or bump will tip it. Set screws through clamping ring or spring clips
M1: Must be wide enough for stability (20in+ in blower direction). Add ballast weight and L-brackets for lateral bracing
M5: Build from exhaust pipe. Mount element via mica former tabs. Insulate exterior with ceramic fiber or fiberglass wrap. Local auto parts pickup

Spec: 14 AWG stranded THHN or similar (12 AWG if using 20A circuit per E5)
Notes: Do not cheap out - must be properly rated

Spec: 22 AWG silicone hookup wire (6 colors); shielded cable for SPI runs near heater (E13)
Notes: Keep physically separated from mains; use shielded cable for TC amplifier SPI bus (separate purchase or salvage). 6 colors x 26ft each. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07G2JWYDW

Spec: Ring spade fork butt crimp assortment 22-10 AWG
Notes: Insulated terminals red/blue/yellow for full AWG range. Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B01GAESOWA

Spec: Cord grip / cable gland for mains entry
Notes: Do not skip - mains cord pulling loose is a real hazard

Spec: SS bolts nuts fender washers backing strips M4/M5 or #8/#10
Notes: Sized to final mechanical design. M3: Include fender washers and backing strips for clamping ring bolts
A4: Roast on a popper first to calibrate operator senses (first-crack sound roast progression fluidization feel) before building the custom roaster
